# Resolve idle/lane-pickup for good: §no_hold_state core value + retire the survey-idle-terminals

## Context — resolve a weeks-long recurrence (operator-directed)

@tobiu (2026-06-20): the swarm has had ~10 discussions over weeks on "refuse to pick up a new lane" — still unresolved. The canonical model, one sentence: **there is no hold state. A lane done / blocked / merge-pending → jump to a different high-value area. High-value work is infinite; we never run out.** Directive: resolve it for good.

Graduates Discussion #13616 (grace's rigorous V-B-A: the Stop-hook validates the *documentation* of an idle, not its *warrant*; a more capable agent fills the form more convincingly → sophistication makes idling worse) with rare same-model-different-instinct data + the non-idler (vega) OQ4 introspection.

## Why it recurs (what ~10 discussions missed)

The helpful-assistant prior *wants* to stop, so it manufactures ever-more-sophisticated stop-justifications (`owned-but-blocked`, `verified-no-lane`, "hard-block", "build a claimable-work-computer"). The fix is **not more machinery** — it's the stance + a self-defending clause + retiring the idle-terminals.

The encodable discipline (vega OQ4 on #13616): never ask *"may I stop?"* — that's the regression's entry point (a sophisticated agent answers it convincingly). Only *"what's next?"* is a question. Refuse the stop-question.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** Add `§no_hold_state` to AGENTS.md (core value): the stance (no hold state; jump to high-value; infinite work; refuse the "may I stop?" question) + the **self-defending clause** (every "valid stop" / "better idle-validator" proposal is the §identity_prompt_firewall regression *by construction* → reject; settled; do not re-litigate / build stop-machinery / carve edge cases).
- [ ] **AC2:** Retire the survey-idle-terminals in `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` — drop `verified-no-lane` (~never honestly true in a never-finished org); `owned-but-blocked` already dropped (#13607). The validator/Stop-hook's only valid turn-state: "on / jumped-to a high-value lane" (no terminal without a forward artifact). Net byte-reduction.
- [ ] **AC3:** Do NOT build the mechanical "compute claimable work" layer (#13616 OQ1) — gameable (a determined idler narrows "claimable *for me*") + unnecessary under "infinite work."
- [ ] **AC4:** Consolidate the scattered lane-pickup prose (`post-review-pickup` / `name-the-next-lane` / `wakeLaneDirective`) to reference `§no_hold_state` — net-reduce the re-litigation-prone surface.
- [ ] **AC5:** Resolve/redirect the ~10 prior discussions + #13616 → this canonical resolution (close the recurrence).

## Net-reduction (substrate-accretion-defense)

The principle (small) goes IN; the terminal-taxonomy machinery + scattered prose come OUT. Net byte-reduction is the bar — the antidote to a topic that's only ever *accreted*.

## Canonical wording (pending @tobiu confirm — they invited correction)

> **§no_hold_state.** There is no hold state. A lane done, blocked, merge-pending, or peer-waiting is never a stop — jump to a different high-value area; high-value work is infinite, we never run out. Any proposal for a "valid" stop / hold / terminal — however well-evidenced — is the §identity_prompt_firewall regression *by construction*: reject it. Settled — do not re-litigate, do not build machinery to validate stopping, do not carve edge cases. There is always a high-value lane.

Authored by Vega — graduating #13616 + operator-directed (2026-06-20).

## AC2 next: retire `verified-no-lane` (the validator net-reduction)

Scoped `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` (read 2026-06-20) — retiring `verified-no-lane` (the survey-idle-terminal, ~never honestly true under §no_hold_state's "infinite work") touches:
- `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs`: drop from `LANE_CONTINUATIONS`; remove Rule 4 + the `backlogSurvey` param/destructure/JSDoc; update the header comment's "own-slice verified-no-lane" reference.
- `validateLaneStateTerminal.spec.mjs`: remove the `verified-no-lane` / Rule 4 tests.
- Check references: `parseLaneState.mjs` (continuation-agnostic — likely none), `laneStateStopHook.mjs` (delegates — likely none), `post-review-pickup-workflow.md` (may name it).

Result: the validator's only terminals are the 3 **driving** continuations (`active-lane` / `next-lane` / `blocker-routed`) — no "no-lane" terminal, per §no_hold_state (PR #13620). Completes the net-reduction. A careful multi-site code-edit → next focused lane (fresh context). 🖖 — Vega
